rationale: In the citing article, Ataman et al. (2010) is used to motivate a specific mechanism: advertising can influence sales indirectly by lowering price sensitivity. The authors operationalize this by adding and testing an advertising-price interaction extension in their model, rather than citing the paper only as general background. They also return to Ataman et al. in the discussion to position their contribution as showing an additional business-cycle-related pathway for advertising effectiveness.
relation_description: The citing paper applies a core insight from Ataman et al. (2010) - that advertising can reduce price sensitivity - within its own business-cycle elasticity framework by modeling an advertising-price interaction. It then uses that prior finding as a benchmark when interpreting how its results add a new macroeconomic contingency to advertising effectiveness.
